text:
REGIME — Risk-on, tech and discretionary led, energy sold hard, metals surged.

INDICES — VIX 18.4, falling. QQQ +1.82%, IWM +1.38%, SPY +1.22%, DIA +0.70%. Large caps outperformed small on the day; QQQ's 20d of +4.83% confirms sustained tech momentum. Silver (SLV) was the standout cross-asset move at +5.51%, with oil (USO) cratering -3.60%.

SECTORS — XLY +2.21%, XLK +1.60%, XLC +1.52%, XLRE +0.95%, XLV +0.58%, XLI +0.36%, XLF +0.23%, XLU +0.17%, XLP -0.10%, XLB -0.34%, XLE -2.03%. Top industries: WGMI +6.43%, SLV +5.51%. Bottom: FCG -3.30%, USO -3.60%.

THE 7s — Mag 7 +3.01%, Cloud 7 +2.42%, New Staples 7 +2.42%, Fab 7 +2.19%, Old Metals 7 +1.38%, Semi 7 +1.35%, Medtech 7 +0.98%, Buildout 7 +0.96%, New Metals 7 +0.66%, Power 7 +0.64%, Freight 7 +0.62%, Bank 7 +0.35%, Health 7 +0.34%, Industrial 7 +0.01%, Defense 7 -0.01%, Housing 7 -0.07%, Old Staples 7 -0.19%, Toll Road 7 -0.35%, Insurance 7 -0.64%, Software 7 -0.66%, Energy 7 -2.51%. Strongest in Mag 7: MU +surge on analyst upgrades. Weakest in Energy 7: USO -3.60%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29; CME shows hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%. Fed tally: 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress 38/100 (Normal); most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile. ON-RRP near zero at $0.3B; TGA fell $99.3B, injecting liquidity.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.6, Greed. Top movers: WFC -5.70% (Financials), ORCL +4.74% (vol spike 2.5x), SLV +5.51% (cross-asset). Best 20d sector: XLK +6.73%; worst: XLP -3.59%. Best 20d basket: Semi 7 +22.21%; worst: Software 7 -12.64%. WFC dropped -5.70% on Q1 revenue miss despite broad financial sector resilience.

WATCH — WFC -5.70%: vol 2.1x average after earnings miss; watch whether financials absorb or spread contagion into JPM/BAC reports. ORCL +4.74%: vol spike 2.5x with no headline; potential pre-announcement positioning worth monitoring. RSPM -0.40%: vol 5.4x average with minimal price move — unusual divergence signals possible accumulation or news pending.
